toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-66cf98cdfb444490880f48828015b7e3 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --verbosity quiet 2>&1
arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage via coverlet

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage via coverlet

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage using coverlet (which is already referenced in the test project) and then analyze the results.

The project has:
- `StudentService.cs` with 4 methods: `Enroll`, `CalculateGpa`, `FindById`, `Search`
- Tests cover `Enroll` (partially), `FindById`, and `Search` (partially)
- `CalculateGpa` has NO tests at all (noted in the test file)
- Several branches in `Enroll` are untested (age validation, duplicate check)
- `Search` with a matching query is untested


Let me execute the tests with coverage collection to see exactly which lines and branches are being missed.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-66cf98cdfb444490880f48828015b7e3 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --verbosity quiet 2>&1
arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage via coverlet
